understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to move a controllable piece (or pieces) through a small pixel-grid world to reach a target location or configuration, while managing the constraints of walls, blocks, and the 3-pixel step mechanic.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement works on a grid where each action shifts the selected piece by exactly 3 pixels (one STEP) in the chosen direction. Adjacent pushable objects can be moved along with the selected piece if you move into them. Walls and boundaries block direct movement, though their exact blocking rules differ between direct movement and pushing. SELECT allows switching control between different objects. Movement hints (blocked flags) indicate adjacent obstacles but don't guarantee whether an action will succeed—actual outcomes must be observed to learn the precise rules.
